reasoning: The total Federal Reserve Reverse Repurchase Agreements (WLRRAL) series has hit a firm floor. Analysis of recent news shows the Overnight Reverse Repo (ON RRP) facility has basically drained to near-zero (approx. $6 billion in early January), acting as a buffer depletion. Thus, the remaining baseline is dominated by the stable Foreign Repo Pool, which hovers around ~$320 billion. This explains why the series dropped to ~317k-327k range in January 2026 and has effectively stalled its long-term negative downward trend. The most recent available data before Feb 1 is the 2026-01-28 observation of $327,151M. Because this base value is slightly above the absolute ~317k floor seen a few weeks prior, near-term fluctuations have a modestly higher chance of dipping down than spiking up in non-month-end periods. Therefore, the near-term forecast (Feb 8 resolution, matching the Feb 4 data point) has a probability just below 50%. The later date (Mar 3 resolution, corresponding to the Feb 25 data point) has more time for variance, so its probability regresses closer to 0.5 due to the random-walk nature of the stable Foreign Repo Pool baseline at this zero-bound of ON RRP.
